scope: wave2 fan-out migration, PARTIAL (gap-closure pass): 'usage', 'tags', and 'categories' are ported at Tier 1 via the S4 engine mini-wave's stream.fan_out primitive (one request per configured image_url). 'colors' and 'faces_detections' remain NOT ported — both require a per-record derived key the dialect cannot express (colors: 3 sibling arrays under one object with no multi-array-union primitive; faces_detections: a face_index primary-key component derived from array position, not any field value) — see docs.md Known limits. Legacy stays authoritative for those 2 streams until a Tier-2 hook closes the remaining gap.

excluded.reason: ENGINE_GAP: result.colors nests 3 sibling arrays (overall_colors/foreground_colors/background_colors) that must be unioned into one record stream tagged by color_scope; records.path/keyed_object have no multi-array-union primitive, only a single dotted-path array or object-of-ids. Needs a StreamHook or a new records dialect primitive, out of scope for this Tier-1-only gap-closure pass.

ENGINE_GAP: legacy's primary key ['image_url','face_index'] derives face_index from the record's 0-based POSITION within result.faces[], not from any field value; computed_fields/records extraction have no array-index accessor, so this PK component cannot be reconstructed without changing emitted record data. Needs a StreamHook or a new records dialect primitive, out of scope for this Tier-1-only gap-closure pass.
